title: Initialize Python project with pyproject.toml
description: Create pyproject.toml with FastAPI, uvicorn, gunicorn, pydantic v2, jinja2, redis, neo4j, zep-python, mcp, langgraph, google-genai, google-cloud-aiplatform, networkx, ragas, pytest, httpx, playwright dev dependencies. Configure ruff and mypy.
steps: Create pyproject.toml with [project] metadata and all runtime dependencies pinned to compatible ranges
Run pip install -e '.[dev]' and verify zero resolution errors
Add [tool.ruff] and [tool.mypy] baseline configuration sections

title: FastAPI application factory
description: Implement app/main.py with create_app() factory registering lifespan hooks, CORS disabled (same-origin HTMX), and route inclusion stubs for chat, cart, checkout, health, and partials.
steps: Create app/main.py with FastAPI create_app() returning configured application
Wire lifespan async context manager for Redis/Neo4j/Zep connection startup and shutdown
Run uvicorn app.main:app --reload and verify GET / returns 200 or redirect to chat

title: Pydantic Settings and environment validation
description: Create app/config.py with pydantic-settings BaseSettings loading REDIS_URL, NEO4J_URI, NEO4J_USER, NEO4J_PASSWORD, ZEP_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, GCP_PROJECT_ID, GCP_LOCATION, KAPRUKA_MCP_URL, and SESSION_SECRET from environment.
steps: Define Settings class with Field validators for required production keys
Add get_settings() lru_cache singleton
Write pytest test_settings_loads_from_env verifying all fields parse correctly

title: Environment example file
description: Create .env.example documenting all required and optional environment variables for local dev and Cloud Run deployment with Memorystore Redis, Neo4j AuraDB, and Zep Cloud.
steps: Create .env.example with commented descriptions for each variable
Document Memorystore VPC connector requirement for Cloud Run
Verify no real secrets are committed; add .env to .gitignore

title: Jinja2 template environment
description: Configure Jinja2Templates in app/templating.py with templates/ directory, auto_reload in dev, and custom filters stub for currency formatting.
steps: Create app/templating.py exposing get_templates() dependency
Create templates/base.html shell with HTMX, Alpine.js, and SSE extension script tags
Write pytest verifying TemplateResponse renders base.html without errors

title: Tailwind CSS standalone CLI build
description: Set up static/css/input.css with Tailwind directives and a Makefile or package script invoking tailwindcss CLI to compile into static/css/app.css for production.
steps: Create static/css/input.css with @tailwind base/components/utilities
Add tailwind.config.js with Kapruka-adjacent warm commerce color palette
Run tailwindcss build and verify static/css/app.css is generated with utility classes

title: Static assets mount
description: Mount StaticFiles at /static in FastAPI serving compiled CSS and any future assets from static/ directory.
steps: Register StaticFiles(directory='static') at /static in create_app()
Verify GET /static/css/app.css returns 200 with text/css content-type
Confirm base.html links to /static/css/app.css

title: Async Redis client wrapper
description: Implement lib/redis/client.py using redis-py async client with connection pooling, reconnect logic, and ping health check against Memorystore Redis URL.
steps: Create RedisClient class wrapping redis.asyncio.from_url with pool settings
Implement async ping() returning bool and async close() for lifespan
Write integration test with fakeredis or docker redis verifying ping succeeds

title: Sliding-window rate limiter per client IP
description: Implement lib/redis/rate_limit.py with token bucket sliding window: 60 requests/minute across all MCP tools and 30 kapruka_create_order calls/hour per client IP using Redis sorted sets.
steps: Implement check_rate_limit(ip, tool_name) raising RateLimitExceeded with retry_after_seconds
Use separate Redis keys rate:{ip}:global and rate:{ip}:create_order
Write pytest simulating 61st global request returns 429 with Retry-After header value

title: Read-only MCP response cache
description: Implement lib/redis/cache.py with 30-minute TTL for read-only Kapruka MCP tools. Cache key format tool:hash(args) using SHA-256 of canonical JSON args. Writes never cached.
steps: Implement get_cached(tool, args) and set_cached(tool, args, response, ttl=1800)
Canonicalize args dict with sorted keys before hashing
Write pytest verifying cache hit on second identical search_products call and bypass for create_order

title: FastAPI dependency injection for Redis
description: Expose get_redis() FastAPI dependency in app/dependencies.py returning the shared RedisClient from app state set during lifespan.
steps: Store RedisClient on app.state.redis in lifespan
Implement get_redis(request) dependency yielding client
Write route test confirming dependency injection resolves without error

title: Neo4j async driver wrapper
description: Implement lib/neo4j/client.py using neo4j Python driver with async session management, connection verify, and health Cypher RETURN 1.
steps: Create Neo4jClient with async execute(cypher, params) helper
Implement health_check() running RETURN 1 AS ok
Write integration test with testcontainers neo4j or mock driver verifying health_check

title: Zep Cloud client wrapper
description: Implement lib/zep/client.py using zep-python SDK with session list/create and health ping against Zep Cloud API.
steps: Create ZepClient wrapping Zep SDK with async-compatible interface
Implement health_check() verifying API key validity
Write unit test with mocked HTTP verifying client initializes with ZEP_API_KEY

title: MCP HTTP JSON-RPC client
description: Implement lib/kapruka/mcp_client.py using mcp Python SDK establishing JSON-RPC connection over HTTP to https://mcp.kapruka.com/mcp with exponential backoff retry on transient failures.
steps: Implement MCPHttpClient.call_tool(name, params) returning raw result string
Add retry decorator with exponential backoff max 3 attempts on 5xx/timeout
Write unit test with httpx mock transport verifying tool call JSON-RPC envelope

title: Kapruka error hierarchy
description: Implement lib/kapruka/errors.py with KaprukaError base, KaprukaRateLimitError (429 with retry_after), KaprukaValidationError, and KaprukaNotFoundError mapped from MCP error strings.
steps: Define exception classes with structured fields code and message
Implement parse_mcp_error(result_str) raising appropriate subclass
Write pytest covering Error (429), empty_cart, and product_not_found parsing

title: Shared Pydantic types for MCP tools
description: Implement lib/kapruka/types.py with Pydantic v2 models for all 7 Kapruka MCP tool inputs and outputs including CartItem, Recipient, Delivery, Sender, CreateOrderResponse with order_ref, checkout_url, expires_at.
steps: Define SearchProductsInput/Output, GetProductInput/Output, ListCategoriesInput/Output models
Define Delivery, Recipient, Sender, CreateOrderInput, CreateOrderResponse with field validators
Write pytest validating gift_message max 300 chars and icing_text max 120 chars constraints

title: Wrapper for kapruka_search_products
description: Implement lib/kapruka/tools/search_products.py calling MCP with response_format json, parsing typed SearchProductsOutput including results, next_cursor, applied_filters.
steps: Implement search_products(q, category, min_price, max_price, in_stock_only, sort, limit, cursor, currency) async function
Force response_format json in MCP params
Write unit test with mocked MCP returning sample JSON verifying parsed ProductResult list

title: Wrapper for kapruka_get_product and kapruka_list_categories
description: Implement lib/kapruka/tools/get_product.py and list_categories.py with typed JSON parsing and response_format json.
steps: Implement get_product(product_id, currency) returning ProductDetail model
Implement list_categories(depth) returning nested CategoryTree model
Write unit tests with fixture JSON for both tools

title: Wrapper for delivery city and check_delivery tools
description: Implement lib/kapruka/tools/delivery.py with list_delivery_cities(query, limit) and check_delivery(city, delivery_date, product_id) typed wrappers.
steps: Implement list_delivery_cities returning list of city name strings
Implement check_delivery validating YYYY-MM-DD date format and returning availability bool
Write unit tests covering city_not_deliverable and date_not_deliverable error parsing

title: Wrapper for kapruka_create_order
description: Implement lib/kapruka/tools/create_order.py building CreateOrderInput from cart, recipient, delivery, sender, gift_message, currency. Parse JSON response with order_ref, checkout_url, expires_at ISO 8601, and summary totals. Sender schema accepts name and anonymous only.
steps: Implement create_order() forcing response_format json and bypassing read cache
Validate cart 1-30 items, quantity 1-99, gift_message max 300, icing_text cakes only max 120
Write unit test verifying parsed CreateOrderResponse fields match MCP JSON schema

title: Wrapper for kapruka_track_order
description: Implement lib/kapruka/tools/track_order.py accepting order_number (post-payment identifier distinct from order_ref) and returning typed tracking status.
steps: Implement track_order(order_number) with response_format json
Define TrackOrderResponse model with status, events, and delivery ETA fields
Write unit test with mocked tracking JSON response

title: KaprukaService facade with cache and rate limiting
description: Implement lib/kapruka/service.py as unified facade delegating to all 7 tool wrappers, applying per-IP rate limits and read cache before MCP calls.
steps: Create KaprukaService class accepting redis client and mcp client in constructor
Wire check_rate_limit before every call; apply cache on read tools only
Write integration test verifying 30-min cache hit and create_order bypasses cache

title: Chat viewport Jinja template with empty state
description: Create templates/chat/index.html as full-screen chat viewport with welcome empty state, message list container div#chat-messages, and Kapruka-adjacent warm commerce styling via Tailwind.
steps: Create templates/chat/index.html extending base.html with centered chat layout
Add empty state placeholder with suggested prompts for gift discovery
Verify GET /chat renders 200 HTML with empty state visible

title: HTMX chat input form
description: Add chat input form with hx-post to /chat/stream, hx-ext sse, hx-target #chat-messages, and hx-swap beforeend for message injection.
steps: Add form with textarea name=message and submit button with hx-post /chat/stream
Configure hx-trigger submit and hx-indicator for loading spinner
Manual test: submit message triggers POST without full page reload

title: Alpine.js focus and scroll helpers
description: Add minimal Alpine.js x-data chatHelpers component for auto-scrolling message list to bottom on new content and focusing input after response completes.
steps: Add x-data chatHelpers with scrollToBottom() method on message container
Wire htmx:afterSwap event listener to call scrollToBottom
Verify in browser that new messages auto-scroll into view

title: AgentState TypedDict schema
description: Define graphs/state.py AgentState with messages, intent, hybrid_context, tool_calls, tool_results, tool_call_count, model_tier, session_id, zep_thread_id, currency, checkout_state, and response_html fields.
steps: Create AgentState TypedDict with all required fields and Optional annotations
Add reducer annotations for messages list using operator.add
Write unit test instantiating minimal AgentState dict passes type check

title: Redis LangGraph checkpointer
description: Configure LangGraph Redis checkpointer in lib/redis/checkpointer.py persisting graph state to Memorystore Redis for conversation continuity across requests.
steps: Implement get_checkpointer(redis_client) returning AsyncRedisSaver or equivalent
Wire checkpointer into shopping graph compile step
Write integration test verifying state persists across two graph invocations with same thread_id

title: analyze_intent LangGraph node
description: Implement graphs/nodes/analyze_intent.py node classifying user message into discovery, checkout, tracking, or general intent using Gemini 2.5 Flash via google-genai SDK.
steps: Implement async analyze_intent(state) returning updated intent field
Use structured output or JSON mode for intent classification
Write unit test with mocked genai client verifying discovery intent for 'birthday cake for mom'

title: retrieve_hybrid_context stub node
description: Implement graphs/nodes/retrieve_hybrid_context.py stub returning empty hybrid_context dict; full Neo4j implementation deferred to Sprint 3 PRD-047.
steps: Create async retrieve_hybrid_context(state) returning state with empty hybrid_context
Add conditional edge skipping stub when intent is tracking or checkout
Write unit test confirming stub returns without Neo4j connection

title: call_mcp_tools LangGraph node
description: Implement graphs/nodes/call_mcp_tools.py selecting and invoking KaprukaService tools based on intent and LLM tool-call decisions, incrementing tool_call_count.
steps: Implement tool selection logic for search_products, get_product, list_categories based on intent
Increment tool_call_count on each invocation; store results in tool_results
Write unit test with mocked KaprukaService verifying search called for discovery intent

title: generate_response LangGraph node
description: Implement graphs/nodes/generate_response.py synthesizing final assistant response with Gemini using MCP tool results only, rendering Jinja message partial to response_html.
steps: Call google-genai with tool_results context; enforce faithfulness to MCP data
Render templates/chat/message_assistant.html with response text
Write unit test verifying response_html contains product names from mocked tool_results

title: Model router Flash to Pro escalation
description: Implement graphs/model_router.py selecting gemini-2.5-flash by default and escalating to gemini-2.5-pro when checkout review turn detected or tool_call_count exceeds 3.
steps: Implement select_model(state) returning model name string
Escalate to pro when state.checkout_state == 'review' or tool_call_count > 3
Write unit test verifying pro selected after 4th tool call

title: Compile main shopping StateGraph
description: Implement graphs/shopping_graph.py wiring analyze_intent, retrieve_hybrid_context, call_mcp_tools, generate_response nodes with conditional edges and Redis checkpointer.
steps: Build StateGraph(AgentState) with all four nodes and edge routing by intent
Compile with checkpointer and export get_shopping_graph() factory
Write integration test invoking graph end-to-end with mocked services

title: Zep session create and resume
description: Implement lib/zep/session.py creating Zep memory sessions on first visit and resuming by session cookie thread_id stored in Redis.
steps: Implement get_or_create_session(session_id) returning zep_thread_id
Store session_id to zep_thread_id mapping in Redis with 7-day TTL
Write unit test verifying new session creates Zep thread and existing resumes

title: Zep memory read and write in graph loop
description: Integrate Zep memory.add and memory.get into shopping graph: append user/assistant messages after each turn and inject memory context into analyze_intent and generate_response.
steps: Add zep_memory_write node or side-effect after generate_response
Inject last 10 Zep memory facts into generate_response system prompt
Write integration test verifying message persisted to Zep mock after chat turn

title: Cross-session preference extraction
description: Use Zep memory search to extract user preferences (favorite categories, past occasions, currency) and inject into AgentState for personalized discovery.
steps: Implement extract_preferences(zep_thread_id) returning dict of preference keys
Merge preferences into hybrid_context hints before MCP search
Write unit test with mocked Zep facts verifying category preference applied

title: SSE streaming chat endpoint
description: Implement POST /chat/stream in app/routes/chat.py returning text/event-stream with async generator yielding HTML token chunks from LangGraph graph.astream.
steps: Create StreamingResponse with media_type text/event-stream
Yield SSE events with data field containing HTML fragments per token or chunk
Write httpx async test verifying Content-Type text/event-stream and event stream format

title: HTMX SSE extension wiring
description: Configure base.html with hx-ext='sse' and chat form sse-connect/sse-swap attributes to consume POST /chat/stream SSE events into #chat-messages.
steps: Include htmx-ext-sse.js in base.html
Set sse-connect and sse-swap on chat form or dedicated SSE listener element
Manual browser test verifying streamed tokens appear incrementally in chat

title: Assistant message Jinja partial
description: Create templates/chat/message_assistant.html partial for hx-swap rendering assistant bubbles with optional embedded product card slots.
steps: Create partial with role=assistant styling and prose Tailwind classes
Support optional products block for inline carousel injection
Render partial in isolation test verifying HTML structure

title: Chat route integration test
description: Write tests/integration/test_chat_flow.py end-to-end test posting message to /chat/stream with mocked KaprukaService and genai, verifying HTML response contains assistant message.
steps: Use httpx AsyncClient with mocked dependencies
POST message='show me birthday cakes' and collect SSE stream
Assert response contains assistant role markup and no unhandled 500 errors

title: Neo4j ontology Cypher schema
description: Define lib/neo4j/ontology.py Cypher constraints and indexes for Occasion, Category, ProductType nodes with OCCASION_TO_CATEGORY and CATEGORY_TO_PRODUCT_TYPE relationships.
steps: Write CREATE CONSTRAINT statements for unique node ids
Define relationship types and property schemas in ontology.py
Run migration script against test Neo4j verifying constraints created

title: Category ingestion script
description: Implement scripts/ingest_categories.py calling kapruka_list_categories via KaprukaService and MERGE-ing Occasion, Category, ProductType triplet nodes into Neo4j.
steps: Fetch categories with depth=3 from live or mocked MCP
Map taxonomy to triplet structure and execute MERGE Cypher batches
Run script and verify node counts with MATCH (n) RETURN labels(n), count(*)

title: Ontology node property enrichment
description: Enrich ingested nodes with slug, display_name, and description properties derived from Kapruka category metadata for embedding and display.
steps: Add property mapping in ingest_categories.py for each node type
Store source kapruka_id on each node for traceability
Verify sample Birthday occasion node has display_name and slug properties

title: Vertex AI text-embedding-005 client
description: Implement lib/embeddings/vertex_embeddings.py using google-cloud-aiplatform to embed text via text-embedding-005 model with batch support and GCP project/location from Settings.
steps: Implement embed_texts(texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]] async wrapper
Configure GCP_PROJECT_ID and GCP_LOCATION from Settings
Write unit test with mocked aiplatform verifying 768-dim vector returned

title: Batch embed ontology nodes
description: Extend ingest_categories.py or add scripts/embed_ontology.py to batch-embed all Occasion, Category, ProductType display_name+description and store embedding property on nodes.
steps: Query all nodes missing embedding property
Batch embed via Vertex client and SET n.embedding = $vector
Verify MATCH (c:Category) WHERE c.embedding IS NOT NULL RETURN count(c) > 0

title: Neo4j vector index creation and query
description: Implement lib/neo4j/vector_search.py creating vector index on Category.embedding and querying via db.index.vector.queryNodes Cypher procedure.
steps: Create vector index ontology_category_embedding with appropriate dimensions and similarity function
Implement vector_search(query_embedding, top_k=5) returning node ids and scores
Write integration test verifying semantically similar category returned for 'birthday gift' query

title: HybridRAG retrieve_hybrid_context full implementation
description: Replace stub in graphs/nodes/retrieve_hybrid_context.py: embed user query via Vertex, Neo4j vector search, 2-hop Cypher traversal Occasion-Category-ProductType, return structured hints for MCP filters.
steps: Embed user message and run vector_search top 5 categories
Traverse 2 hops via lib/neo4j/traverse.py collecting related occasions and product types
Write integration test verifying hybrid_context contains category filter for 'wedding flowers'

title: Neo4j multi-hop traversal helper
description: Implement lib/neo4j/traverse.py executing parameterized 2-hop Cypher from seed category nodes returning connected occasions, categories, and product types with relationship weights.
steps: Implement traverse_from_categories(category_ids, max_hops=2) returning TraversalResult
Include relationship type and hop depth in result
Write unit test with fixture graph verifying 2-hop reachability

title: Graph-informed MCP filter injection
description: Update call_mcp_tools node to pass hybrid_context category and occasion hints as category filter and refined q parameters to kapruka_search_products.
steps: Map top traversal category to search_products category param
Augment q with occasion keywords when confidence score exceeds threshold
Write unit test verifying search called with category='Birthday' for birthday intent context

title: Product card Jinja partial
description: Create templates/components/product_card.html rendering product image, name, price with format_currency, in_stock badge, and hx-post add-to-cart button.
steps: Accept product dict with id, name, price, image_url, in_stock, url fields
Render Tailwind card with hover state and stock badge overlay
Snapshot test rendering sample product fixture HTML

title: Horizontal product carousel partial
description: Create templates/components/product_carousel.html with horizontal scroll container including multiple product_card partials and scroll snap CSS.
steps: Render list of products as horizontal flex overflow-x-auto carousel
Include prev/next Alpine buttons for keyboard-accessible navigation
Verify carousel renders 3+ cards without layout overflow on mobile viewport

title: Category filter chips partial
description: Create templates/components/category_chips.html rendering clickable HTMX filter chips from hybrid_context categories triggering refined search partial swap.
steps: Render chips with hx-get /partials/search?category={name} hx-target #results
Highlight active chip with distinct Tailwind variant
Test chip click triggers HTMX request without full page reload

title: Stock badge overlay component
description: Create templates/components/stock_badge.html partial showing in-stock green badge or out-of-stock muted overlay based on in_stock and stock_level fields.
steps: Render green 'In Stock' badge when in_stock true
Render gray 'Out of Stock' overlay when in_stock false
Include partial in product_card and verify both states render correctly

title: Alpine lazy-load image animations
description: Add Alpine.js x-data lazyImage component with intersection observer fading in product images on carousel scroll into viewport.
steps: Implement lazyImage with loading skeleton and opacity transition on load
Apply to product_card img tags in carousel
Verify images fade in on scroll in browser devtools throttled network

title: Embed product carousel in assistant messages
description: Update generate_response node and message_assistant.html to inject product_carousel partial when tool_results contain search_products results.
steps: Detect search results in tool_results and pass products list to Jinja context
Render carousel below assistant text in message partial
Integration test verifying carousel HTML present after product search turn

title: Currency formatting Jinja filter
description: Implement lib/utils/currency.py format_currency(amount, currency) supporting LKR, USD, GBP, AUD, CAD, EUR and register as Jinja filter in templating.py.
steps: Format LKR without decimals and USD/EUR with two decimal places
Register format_currency filter in Jinja environment
Write unit test: format_currency(1500, 'LKR') returns 'Rs. 1,500'

title: Header currency selector with HTMX
description: Add persistent header currency select with hx-post /session/currency, hx-swap none, options LKR USD GBP AUD CAD EUR, defaulting to session currency from Redis.
steps: Render select in base.html header with current currency selected
POST updates Redis session currency key session:{id}:currency
Verify currency change persists across page refresh within same session

title: Propagate session currency to all MCP calls
description: Inject session currency from Redis into AgentState.currency at graph start and pass to every KaprukaService call including search, get_product, and create_order.
steps: Load currency in chat route before graph invocation
Pass currency param in all MCP tool calls via call_mcp_tools node
Write test verifying search_products called with currency='USD' after session update

title: Redis server-side cart state
description: Implement lib/redis/cart.py storing cart matrix in Redis hash cart:{session_id} with up to 30 items, each with product_id, quantity, icing_text, name, price snapshot.
steps: Implement add_item, remove_item, update_quantity, get_cart, clear_cart methods
Enforce max 30 items and quantity 1-99 per item
Write unit test adding 31st item raises CartLimitExceeded

title: Cart HTMX partial swaps
description: Implement app/routes/cart.py with hx-post /cart/add, /cart/remove, /cart/update returning templates/checkout/cart_partial.html fragments for hx-swap outerHTML on #cart-panel.
steps: Create cart_partial.html listing items with quantity stepper and remove buttons
Each action returns updated partial without full page reload
Integration test POST /cart/add returns HTML with product name

title: Alpine cart drawer
description: Add Alpine.js cart drawer slide-over in base.html toggled by cart icon badge showing item count from cart partial hx-swap.
steps: Implement x-data cartDrawer with open/close state and transition classes
Cart icon shows badge count from server-rendered cart item total
Verify drawer opens/closes with escape key and backdrop click

title: Delivery city debounced autocomplete
description: Create templates/checkout/delivery_city.html with hx-get /partials/delivery-cities, hx-trigger keyup changed delay:300ms, calling kapruka_list_delivery_cities for autocomplete suggestions.
steps: Implement partial route returning li suggestion items for hx-swap
Debounce input with hx-trigger delay:300ms
Integration test typing 'Col' returns Colombo suggestion HTML

title: Colombo timezone delivery date picker
description: Create templates/checkout/delivery_date.html with date input min=today in Asia/Colombo timezone and hx-post /checkout/check-delivery validating via kapruka_check_delivery.
steps: Compute today date in Asia/Colombo using zoneinfo
POST selected date and city to check_delivery endpoint returning availability partial
Write test verifying past date rejected with user-friendly error partial

title: Delivery address and instructions form
description: Create templates/checkout/delivery_form.html with address, city, location_type select (house/apartment/office/other), date, and optional instructions fields with Pydantic validation.
steps: Validate address 3-250 chars and instructions max 250 chars server-side
Render inline field errors as HTMX OOB swap fragments
Test invalid address returns error partial without losing form state

title: Recipient name and phone validation
description: Create templates/checkout/recipient_form.html validating recipient name 1-80 chars and phone in E.164 +9477 or local 077 Sri Lanka formats via Pydantic validator.
steps: Implement phone regex validator accepting +94771234567 and 0771234567 formats
Return field-level error partials on validation failure
Write pytest for valid and invalid phone numbers

title: Sender form with anonymous toggle
description: Create templates/checkout/sender_form.html with sender name field and anonymous checkbox only. No email field per MCP Sender schema.
steps: Render name input max 80 chars and anonymous boolean toggle
When anonymous checked, display preview showing 'Anonymous' on gift card
Verify form schema matches Sender Pydantic model with name and anonymous only

title: Checkout sub-graph state machine definition
description: Implement graphs/checkout_graph.py as deterministic LangGraph sub-graph with strict states: cart, delivery_city, delivery_date, recipient, sender, review. No backward skips without explicit user action.
steps: Define CheckoutState TypedDict with current_step and form field accumulators
Add nodes for each step with validation gates blocking advance on invalid data
Write unit test verifying cannot jump from cart to review without intermediate steps

title: Checkout sub-graph integration with main graph
description: Wire checkout_graph as conditional sub-graph invoked when analyze_intent classifies checkout intent or user clicks proceed-to-checkout from cart drawer.
steps: Add conditional edge from analyze_intent to checkout_graph when intent=checkout
Pass cart state from Redis into checkout_graph initial state
Integration test verifying checkout flow starts from cart with items present

title: Checkout review step with Pro model
description: Implement review node in checkout_graph rendering order summary partial and escalating to Gemini 2.5 Pro via model_router for final confirmation turn.
steps: Render templates/checkout/review.html with cart items, delivery, recipient, sender summary
Force model_tier=pro on review step
Verify model_router returns pro when checkout_state is review

title: kapruka_create_order integration
description: Implement checkout finalize node calling KaprukaService.create_order with assembled cart, recipient, delivery, sender, gift_message, currency. Parse order_ref, checkout_url, expires_at from JSON response.
steps: Build CreateOrderInput from checkout state and Redis cart
Parse CreateOrderResponse; store order_ref and expires_at in session Redis
Integration test with mocked MCP verifying checkout_url returned in state

title: Click-to-pay countdown UI component
description: Create templates/checkout/payment_cta.html displaying checkout_url link, order_ref, grand_total, and Alpine.js countdown timer to expires_at ISO 8601 with warning when under 10 minutes.
steps: Parse expires_at and render MM:SS countdown updating every second
Style primary CTA button linking to checkout_url opens in new tab
Verify countdown reaches zero and displays expired message

title: Order tracking chat flow
description: Implement tracking intent handler in call_mcp_tools calling kapruka_track_order with order_number extracted from user message, rendering templates/checkout/tracking_status.html partial.
steps: Extract order_number from user message via regex or LLM structured extraction
Call track_order with post-payment order_number not order_ref
Write unit test verifying tracking partial renders status timeline HTML

title: Exception middleware for MCP errors
description: Implement app/middleware/errors.py mapping KaprukaRateLimitError, KaprukaValidationError, and unhandled exceptions to user-friendly HTMX-compatible HTML error partials.
steps: Register exception handlers returning templates/partials/error_banner.html
Include error code and human-readable message in partial
Write test verifying KaprukaValidationError returns 400 HTML not JSON

title: 429 rate limit alert banner with countdown
description: Extend error middleware for 429 responses rendering HTMX alert banner with Retry-After countdown timer and auto-dismiss when window resets.
steps: Parse Retry-After header into countdown seconds on KaprukaRateLimitError
Render templates/partials/rate_limit_banner.html with Alpine countdown
Write test simulating 429 returns banner with data-retry-after attribute

title: NetworkX community detection background worker
description: Implement lib/analytics/networkx_worker.py as asyncio background task exporting Neo4j co-purchase edges to NetworkX graph and running community detection for frequently-bought-together recommendations.
steps: Query Neo4j for CO_PURCHASED_WITH relationships or synthesize from category proximity
Run networkx.community.louvain_communities in thread executor
Store community recommendations back to Neo4j RECOMMENDS relationships

title: Optional cuGraph CUDA Dockerfile stage
description: Add optional Dockerfile.cuda stage installing cugraph for local GPU development. Document that production Cloud Run uses NetworkX path only. Not enabled in default Cloud Run deploy.
steps: Add Dockerfile.cuda stage with CUDA base image and cugraph pip install
Create lib/analytics/cugraph_optional.py importing cugraph only when CUDA available
Document in README that cuGraph stage is dev-only; Cloud Run uses NetworkX

title: Ragas evaluation golden dataset
description: Create evals/golden_dataset.json with 20+ query-answer pairs covering discovery, checkout guidance, and tracking scenarios with expected MCP tool usage patterns.
steps: Define golden cases with user_query, expected_tools, reference_answer fields
Cover birthday cake search, flower delivery check, and order tracking cases
Validate JSON schema with pytest fixture loader

title: Ragas eval script and CI mock MCP fixture
description: Implement evals/ragas_eval.py measuring context_precision, answer_relevance, and faithfulness against golden dataset with mocked KaprukaService transport for CI.
steps: Implement run_ragas_eval() using ragas metrics on graph outputs
Create tests/fixtures/mcp_mock.py returning deterministic JSON for all 7 tools
Add CI job running ragas_eval with thresholds context_precision >= 0.7

title: Ruff and mypy CI gate
description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml running ruff check, ruff format --check, mypy on app/ lib/ graphs/, and pytest unit tests on every push.
steps: Configure GitHub Actions with Python 3.12 matrix
Run ruff and mypy with zero errors required to pass
Run pytest tests/unit excluding integration tests requiring live services

title: Multi-stage production Dockerfile
description: Create Dockerfile with builder stage installing Python deps and runtime stage with gunicorn+uvicorn, non-root user, PORT=8080, and static CSS copied from build stage.
steps: Builder stage runs pip install and tailwindcss compile
Runtime stage uses python:3.12-slim with gunicorn app.main:app -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker
docker build succeeds and container starts on PORT 8080

title: Aggregated health check endpoint
description: Implement GET /health in app/routes/health.py returning JSON status of Redis ping, Neo4j RETURN 1, Zep health, and MCP lightweight list_categories call with overall healthy/degraded status.
steps: Return 200 with {status: healthy, services: {redis, neo4j, zep, mcp}} when all pass
Return 503 degraded when any critical service fails
Write test with mocked services verifying response schema

title: Gunicorn Uvicorn production entrypoint
description: Create gunicorn.conf.py with worker count, timeout, and bind 0.0.0.0:8080 configuration for Cloud Run. Document Procfile or CMD in Dockerfile.
steps: Configure workers = (2 * cpu_count) + 1 and timeout 120 for SSE streams
Set graceful_timeout and keepalive for Cloud Run request patterns
Verify gunicorn starts and serves /health with 200

title: Google Cloud Run deployment with gcloud
description: Create scripts/deploy_cloud_run.sh and docs/DEPLOY.md with step-by-step gcloud commands: build image, push to Artifact Registry, deploy Cloud Run with VPC connector for Memorystore, Secret Manager env vars.
steps: Document gcloud artifacts repositories create and docker push steps
Document gcloud run deploy with --vpc-connector, --set-secrets, --min-instances 0
Include env var checklist for REDIS_URL, NEO4J_URI, ZEP_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, GCP_PROJECT_ID

title: Playwright E2E smoke tests
description: Implement tests/e2e/test_smoke.py with Playwright testing chat page load, product search flow with mocked MCP, cart add partial, and checkout form render stopping before real create_order.
steps: Configure pytest-playwright with base_url http://localhost:8080
Test: load /chat, submit search message, verify product card appears in DOM
Test: add to cart and verify cart drawer shows item; mock create_order in test env
